regime_justification: USDJPY at 160.5, 47 pips below 160 watch line, with BOJ meeting in 4 days priced for 1% hike but governor hospitalized; vol elevated (VIX 22, up 12% yesterday); intervention rhetoric active but no action yet.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY at historic intervention threshold (160.5), MOF verbal warnings active, BOJ meeting June 15-16 with 80-96% hike probability, FX vol bid (1M ~7-8%), JGB 10Y at 2.69% (near 29Y high), risk-off cross-asset (Nikkei -1.9%, VIX +12%).

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: BOJ June 15-16 meeting priced for 1% hike but Ueda absent; if delivered = JPY rally + carry unwind signatures; USDJPY at 160.5 = MOF intervention zone.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Post-BOJ positioning window; if hike + hawkish forward guidance, spread compresses 25-40 bps and vol stays elevated; watch ETF outflows and HY spreads.

3w.label: Unlikely
3w.driver: Market digests June BOJ outcome; assuming orderly hike, acute unwind risk fades unless Iran conflict escalates or US data forces Fed repricing.

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: Base case: gradual normalization path established, carry adjusts but doesn't break; tail risk if BOJ signals faster pace or second hike by Sept.

bottom_line: Stage 2: Pre-Intervention. USDJPY at 160.5, 47 pips from peak intervention threshold. BOJ meets June 15-16 with 80-96% priced probability of hike to 1.0%, but Governor Ueda hospitalized and will miss the meeting for first time since 1998 framework—succession risk now live. Japan PPI 6.1% YoY (fastest in 3Y), JGB 10Y 2.69% (near 29Y high), vol spiking (VIX +12% to 22, MOVE 77). Iran-US strikes ongoing, oil ~$90 WTI. Grades: 1w/2w C (BOJ catalyst), 3w/4w D (orderly path). Crypto draining (BTC $62k, down 50% from Oct peak, ETF outflows 12 sessions). Watch June 15-16 decision, deputy leadership dynamics, and post-hike guidance for pace. Applied unwind prior to 1w/2w grades—ambiguous setup (hike priced but Ueda absent, vol elevated but equities holding) tilts watchful not unlikely given proximity to known catalyst.

trigger_map.trigger: BOJ rate hike surprise / accelerated normalization
trigger_map.note: June 15-16 meeting: 80-96% priced for 1.0%; Ueda absent (hospitalized)—deputies lead; risk: hawkish guidance signals Sept hike or faster QT taper.
USDJPY 160.5 = watch line; Katayama verbal warnings active ("decisive action"); rate-checks not reported; likely waits for post-BOJ repricing unless pace accelerates.
Currently 2.69%; touched 2.8% May 18; fiscal concerns + BOJ taper plan in focus; break above = terminal rate repricing.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl above prior peak
trigger_map.note: ¥/bbl now 14,365 (WTI $89.5 × 160.5); US says strikes "completed" but Hormuz near-closed; PPI 6.1% fastest in 3Y.
Ueda hospitalized June 10, will miss June 15-16 meeting (first absence since 1998); deputies Himino/Uchida lead; market unsure on communication clarity.

narrative.p1: Overnight: USDJPY steady at 160.5, holding 47 pips below the 160.74 May peak and historical MOF intervention threshold. Major headline: BOJ Governor Ueda hospitalized June 10 for infected liver cyst treatment, will miss the June 15-16 policy meeting for the first time since the 1998 decision framework—deputies Himino and Uchida will lead deliberations. Markets widely price 80-96% probability of hike to 1.0% (first time since 1995), but Ueda's absence injects communication risk and succession uncertainty. Japan data: wholesale PPI 6.1% YoY (fastest in 3 years), reinforcing hike case. US-Iran: strikes described as "completed" by US military, oil pullback from $95 Brent to $92, though Hormuz remains near-closed. US CPI 4.2% matched estimates; core 2.9%; Dec Fed hike fully priced. Cross-asset: VIX spiked +12% to 22 (tech selloff + geopolitics), Nikkei -1.9%, BTC bounced to $62k from $61k lows but still down 50% from October peak.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP spread 183 bps (compressed 5 bps overnight), carry-to-vol ~24 (183/7.7), USDJPY 5d pace flat but 30d elevated. Distance from 60d mean: ~60 pips stretched. JGB 10Y at 2.69% is 11 bps shy of the May 18 29-year high of 2.8%; a post-hike break above 2.8% would signal terminal-rate repricing and fiscal stress. Stage 2: Pre-Intervention. USDJPY at the watch line, MOF rhetoric active (Katayama: "decisive action" / Mimura: "final evacuation warning"), but no rate-checks reported—likely waiting for post-BOJ repricing. Grades 1w/2w = C: BOJ meeting is the domino. If 1.0% delivered with forward guidance for another hike in Sept, spread compresses another 25-40 bps and vol stays elevated—early unwind signatures. BUT Ueda's absence is a wildcard: deputies may deliver a mechanical hike with cautious language, reducing immediate catalyst strength. Applied unwind prior here: data ambiguous (hike priced, but will it be hawkish? vol elevated, but equities resilient), proximity to known catalyst tilts C not D. Grades 3w/4w = D: assuming orderly hike, acute risk fades unless Iran re-escalates or Fed data surprises. MOVE at 77 (mid-range) and HY spreads stable suggest cross-asset stress contained for now. Key miss: FX risk reversals and 3M basis unavailable; would clarify hedging demand.
narrative.p3: Crypto: sympathy sell continues. BTC at $62k, down 50% from October $126k all-time high. Spot ETF outflows hit 12 consecutive sessions totaling ~$3.5B—longest streak on record. Strategy Inc (Saylor proxy) sold $2.5M of holdings in June, first divestment since late 2022, signaling shift. Correlation to risk-on broken: equities (S&P) near highs while BTC bleeds, suggesting capital rotation toward AI/tech IPOs (SpaceX, etc.) rather than digital assets. XRP unavailable in search; assume similar -30-50% drawdown. BTC open interest direction: falling (liquidations near $1B this week per search context). BTC-DXY 30d correlation: likely positive (both rising on safe-haven demand), which is unusual—typically inverse. Implication for positioning: crypto is NOT a carry-unwind hedge this cycle; it's draining liquidity alongside yen weakness. If BOJ hikes and USDJPY sells off sharply, expect brief BTC relief rally on risk-on rotation, but structural bid is gone. Avoid over-allocation until BTC reclaims $70k and ETF flows reverse.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1-2 hinge entirely on June 15-16 BOJ decision. Consensus: hike to 1.0% with 80-96% probability. Ueda's absence (hospitalized) means deputies Himino/Uchida lead—historically cautious, may deliver mechanical hike with dovish forward guidance, reducing immediate spread compression. However, if BOJ signals another hike in Sept (3 members already voted for 1.0% in April) or accelerates QT taper beyond the June review plan, spread compresses 25-40 bps rapidly and USDJPY sells off toward 155. That triggers vol spike, Nikkei -5-10%, and early carry unwind signatures. MOF may intervene if USDJPY pace accelerates post-decision (historically reacts to pace, not level). Grade C for both weeks: setup is fragile, catalyst known, but outcome distribution wide.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2-3: market digests BOJ decision. Base case: orderly hike to 1.0%, spread at 150-160 bps, USDJPY range 155-160. Vol elevated but contained (VIX 18-22, MOVE <90). Acute unwind risk fades unless: (1) Iran conflict re-escalates (Hormuz fully closes), spiking oil >$110 and imported inflation, or (2) US payrolls/CPI surprise forces Fed to price in July hike instead of Dec. Both tails <30% probability. Grade drops to D by week 3 assuming no surprises.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 3-4: focus shifts to Fed dots (if FOMC in this window) and July BOJ minutes / Summary of Opinions for clues on Sept hike timing. Carry trade reprices gradually; spread stabilizes 150-170 bps. Unwind risk low unless BOJ signals faster normalization pace (e.g., two more hikes in 2026 instead of one). Tail risk: Ueda's health deteriorates, succession talk accelerates, policy uncertainty spikes. Grade D: benign path unless new catalyst.

watch_list: June 15-16 BOJ decision: hike size (25 bps to 1.0% expected), forward guidance on Sept hike, QT taper pace, deputy communication clarity.
USDJPY pace post-BOJ: if drops >2% in 24h orbreaches 155, MOF intervention probability jumps to >70%; watch for rate-checks.
JGB 10Y: break above 2.8% (May 18 high) = terminal rate repricing + fiscal stress narrative; 30Y auction June 11 for demand clues.
Ueda health updates: prolonged absence or resignation talk = BOJ policy uncertainty premium; watch yen and JGB vol.
US PPI June 11 + jobless claims: if hot, Dec Fed hike pricing strengthens, supports DXY/pressures yen; watch cross-asset correlations.
